summaries.brief_summary: Patrick Ferris opened a PR reworking meio's logging, CLI stdout capture, and task-tree rendering ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76)).
summaries.activity_summary: PR #76 bundles a logging rewrite, a new --stdout CLI flag, and console rendering fixes, still awaiting merge.
summaries.activity:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened **PR #76** ("Fixes"), a bundle of changes to the `meio` CLI, logging internals, and console rendering that has not yet merged ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- The `meio` binary gains a new `--stdout` option to redirect a monitored program's stdout to a file, and the `run` command now accepts an existing PID in addition to spawning a fresh executable and argument list ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- `Logging` is reworked with a `'a log` type and `info`, `warn`, and `debug` helpers that attach a monotonic timestamp tag to each message, and the reporter now also echoes formatted, timestamped log lines to stderr alongside the existing UI queue ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- `Meio.ui` now exposes the `Logging` module and takes a `start` timestamp argument, and `Task.t.logs` changes from a plain string list to a `(bool * string) list` so the console can distinguish user-emitted logs from internal ones ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- Console rendering fixes include truncating long task names to 32 characters (falling back to `<task>` when a name is missing), resizing the `INFO` column, and fixing a bug where task-tree column widths were computed from the wrong tuple element ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
- The `meio.opam` file now pins a minimum OCaml version of 5.4.0, and the bundled examples (`burn.ml`, `burn_domains.ml`, `deadlock.ml`) were updated to match the CLI changes ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))
summaries.trends_summary: Continued incremental, solo-maintained refinement of meio's logging and terminal UI following on from prior CLI work.
summaries.emerging_trends: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) continues iterating on meio's observability features in a single-contributor cadence, following the earlier `Cmdliner`-based CLI rework merged in [#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/75), with this week's [#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76) further extending logging and CLI ergonomics
summaries.contributors_summary: Patrick Ferris authored and opened the week's only pull request, spanning CLI, logging, and console rendering changes.
summaries.contributors: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened **PR #76**, touching 17 files across the `meio` binary, the `Logging`, `Meio`, `Console`, `State`, and `Task` modules, and the bundled examples, adding a `--stdout` capture option, timestamp-tagged logging helpers, and console rendering fixes ([#76](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/pull/76))

summaries.brief_summary: meio CLI migrated to cmdliner with proper argument parsing, help text, man pages, and -- flag disambiguation.
summaries.new_features_summary: Cmdliner-based CLI adds --help, --version, man pages, and -- flag disambiguation for monitored executables.
summaries.new_features:
- The `meio` executable now uses **cmdliner** for argument parsing, replacing the previous bare `Sys.argv.(1)` approach; this brings proper `--help` output, `--version` support (resolved from `%%VERSION%%`), and a man page with an examples section ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- **Positional argument syntax** `meio <executable> [args...]` is now properly handled, with the executable and its arguments parsed as separate positional arguments rather than split on spaces ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- **`--` disambiguation** is now supported, allowing `meio -- <executable> --flag1 --flag2=foo ...` for monitoring Eio programs that themselves take flags ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- The `meio` binary dependency on **`eio_main`** was removed (it now uses `cmdliner` + `unix`); `eio_main` is retained in `meio.opam` but annotated as only needed for examples ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- **ocamlformat** bumped from `0.26.0` to `0.29.0`, with cosmetic reformatting applied to `task_tree.ml` ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
summaries.activity_summary: PR #75 migrating the meio CLI to cmdliner was merged by Patrick Ferris.
summaries.activity: - **cmdliner CLI migration** ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75)) opened by [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) on 2026-05-28 and merged on 2026-06-04; changed 5 files with 49 additions and 19 deletions across the binary entrypoint, dune build file, and opam metadata
summaries.trends_summary: meio is maturing its CLI tooling, reducing ad-hoc argument handling in favour of established OCaml CLI libraries.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- The shift from raw `Sys.argv` parsing to **cmdliner** reflects a push toward conventional OCaml CLI ergonomics, giving `meio` standard `--help`/`--version` behaviour expected by end users ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
- Removal of the **`eio_main`** runtime dependency from the main binary (retaining it only for examples) suggests an ongoing effort to keep the tool's dependency footprint minimal ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75))
summaries.contributors_summary: Patrick Ferris authored and merged the cmdliner CLI migration PR.
summaries.contributors: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) authored [#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75), implementing the cmdliner-based CLI rewrite across `src/bin/meio.ml`, `src/bin/dune`, `meio.opam`, and `.ocamlformat`

summaries.brief_summary: Patrick Ferris opened a cmdliner migration PR and closed three stale PRs to tidy the backlog.
summaries.activity_summary: CLI argument parsing is being migrated to cmdliner, with stale PRs from 2023–2024 closed.
summaries.activity:
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened [#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75) to **migrate the `meio` executable to cmdliner** for argument parsing, taking advantage of a dependency already present in the project and bumping the ocamlformat version alongside it.
- Three **stale PRs were closed without merging**: the 2024 Eio tracing events port ([#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71)), the 2023 OCaml 5.1.0 compatibility and `meio-runtime-events` generalisation ([#67](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/67)), and an earlier attempt at improved usage errors via `Stdlib.Arg` ([#74](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/74)) — all superseded by or conflicting with the current cmdliner approach.
summaries.trends_summary: Consolidation around cmdliner for CLI parsing, replacing earlier Stdlib.Arg and ad-hoc approaches.
summaries.emerging_trends: - The closure of [#74](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/74) (Stdlib.Arg) in favour of [#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75) (cmdliner) points to a deliberate move toward a **richer CLI parsing library** that was already a declared dependency, cleaning up argument handling for the `meio` executable.
summaries.contributors_summary: Patrick Ferris was the sole contributor, opening one new PR and closing three stale ones.
summaries.contributors: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) opened the cmdliner migration PR ([#75](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/75)), and closed three long-standing stale PRs ([#74](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/74), [#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71), [#67](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/67)) to reduce backlog clutter.

summaries.brief_summary: Eio 0.14+ compatibility update migrates meio from the deprecated Ctf API to the new Eio_runtime_events module.
summaries.new_features_summary: Migrated to Eio_runtime_events API and switched from nottui to nottui-unix for the UI loop.
summaries.new_features:
- **Migration from `Eio.Private.Ctf` to `Eio_runtime_events`**: the internal event callback system was rewritten to use the new `Eio_runtime_events` module and its typed `event` variant, replacing the older `Ctf`-based callback approach with a unified `RE.add_callbacks` call ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Switch from `nottui` to `nottui-unix`**: the UI event loop now uses `Nottui_unix.run` instead of `Nottui.Ui_loop.run`, reflecting an upstream split of the nottui library ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **New `eio_name` helper library for examples**: a small `Eio_name` library was extracted into `example/eio_name.ml`, providing a `name` function that sets fiber names via `Eio.Private.Trace.name`, replacing direct use of `Eio.Private.Ctf.set_name` and `Eio.Private.Ctf.log` in the example programs ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **`opam` dependency updates**: `meio.opam` now requires `eio >= 0.14`, `nottui-unix` (replacing `nottui`), and `dune >= 3.10`; the custom git pin-depends on a private eio fork have been removed, enabling installation from the standard opam repository ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Improved task kind display**: `console.ml` now renders task kinds using `Eio_runtime_events.pp_event` for unknown event types, and correctly distinguishes cancellation contexts (`cc`) from fibers (`task`) using the new variant patterns from `Eio_runtime_events.event` ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Removed `Ctf.with_tracing` requirement from examples**: the `burn.ml`, `burn_domains.ml`, `main.ml`, and `zero.ml` examples no longer need to wrap execution in `Ctf.with_tracing`, simplifying the example entry points ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
- **Updated CI to OCaml 5.3 and `setup-ocaml@v3`**: the CI workflow was updated to target OCaml 5.3 (from 5.1.1) and uses the latest `ocaml/setup-ocaml@v3` action, removing the custom opam repository overrides and the custom notty pin ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), [673fdbd](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/commit/673fdbd110a5695b1ed297d938e1a27233464c68))
summaries.activity_summary: PR #72 merging the Eio 0.14+ compatibility work, and issue #73 tracking the disabled screencast CI generation.
summaries.activity:
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) merged [#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) "Eio.0.14+", a **rebased version of [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris)'s earlier work in [#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71)**, bringing the codebase up to date with the modern Eio API surface
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) opened [#73](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/73) "Re-enable the screencast generation in CI", noting that the screencast pipeline depended on a patched version of the `notty` library that hardcoded terminal size; the fix requires either updating the workaround or adding terminal-size configuration upstream — the screencast steps are currently **commented out in CI** pending a solution
summaries.discussions_summary: Patrick Ferris acknowledged the Eio 0.14+ PR merge with a brief thank-you comment.
summaries.notable_discussions: - [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) commented on [#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) thanking [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) for rebasing and landing the Eio 0.14+ compatibility work originally authored in [#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71)
summaries.trends_summary: The project is modernising its Eio dependency and CI infrastructure following upstream Eio and notty library evolution.
summaries.emerging_trends:
- **Eio API modernisation**: the removal of `Eio.Private.Ctf` usage in favour of `Eio_runtime_events` and `Eio.Private.Trace` reflects the stabilisation of the Eio runtime-events interface in Eio 0.14+; meio is tracking these upstream changes to avoid relying on internal private APIs ([#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72))
- **CI infrastructure debt**: the screencast generation pipeline was disabled rather than updated ([#73](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/73)), pointing to a known maintenance gap around terminal-size handling in the `notty` dependency that will need resolution before animated documentation can be regenerated
summaries.contributors_summary: Tim McGilchrist drove the Eio 0.14+ update; Patrick Ferris's prior work in #71 formed the basis of the merged changes.
summaries.contributors:
- [Tim McGilchrist](https://github.com/tmcgilchrist) rebased and merged [#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72) (Eio 0.14+ compatibility, 22 files changed), opened issue [#73](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/73) tracking the screencast CI regression, and landed the CI update to OCaml 5.3 and `setup-ocaml@v3`
- [Patrick Ferris](https://github.com/patricoferris) authored the original Eio 0.14+ compatibility work in [#71](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/71) that was rebased into [#72](https://github.com/ocaml-multicore/meio/issues/72), and commented on the merged PR acknowledging the contribution

summaries.brief_summary: Eio 0.14+ compatibility work opened, older documentation PR closed
summaries.activity_summary: Eio 0.14+ compatibility PR opened with dependency updates across 22 files, older documentation PR closed
summaries.activity:
